product.description: FairPrice Yakitori consists of grilled pieces of tender chicken leg, skewered and marinated with Japanese-style soy sauce. The sweet and savoury glaze, with the charcoal charred fragrance gives it a rich flavour that will keep you wanting more. It is a source of protein, naturally trans fat free and free of artificial preservatives. Enjoy it on its own or accompany it with steamed rice and edamame for a well-rounded meal.

metaData.Ingredients: Chicken Leg, Water, Sugar, Soy Sauce (Water, Defatted Soybeans, Wheat, Salt), High Fructose Corn Syrup, Modified Starches (E1422, E1440), Flavour Enhancers (E621, E631, E627), Colouring (E150A), Humectant [Ins451(I), Ins452(I), Ins450(Iii), Ins340(Ii), Maltodextrin], Salt, Acidity Regulator (E330), Rice Vinegar and Stabiliser (E415).
metaData.Key Information:
• Glazed with Fragrant Soy Sauce
• Juicy & Tender Meat
• Charcoal Grilled
metaData.Preparation:
Cooking instructions:
Do not defrost. Once thawed, do not refreeze.

Microwave:
Without defrosting, place 4 - 5 pieces of Yakitori on a microwavable plate and heat for 2.5 - 3.5 minutes at 800 watts.

Conditions may vary due to different brands of cooking equipment used.
metaData.SAP Product Name: FAIRPRICE CHICKEN YAKITORI WITH SOY SAUCE 250G
metaData.Storage Information:
Keep frozen (-18°C & below).
Once thawed, do not refreeze.

metaData.Ingredients:
• Chicken Satay: Chicken, Sugar, Salt, Turmeric, Garlic, Shallots, Ginger, Lemongrass, Galangal, Coriander, Cumin, Fennel
• Satay Sauce: Peanuts, Water, Vegetable Oil, Sugar, Salt, Chilli, Coconut Milk, Tamarind, Fennel, Cumin, Coriander, Galangal, Garlic, Shallots, Lemongrass, Ginger
metaData.Key Information:
• Traditionally Marinated

• No MSG

• Gravy provided and vacuum packed
metaData.Preparation:
• Cooking Instruction for Pan Fry Satay:
1. Thaw satay meat slices thoroughly
2. Heat 1 tablespoon of oil in a pan
3. Place thawed meat slices in pan and stir fry for 6 minutes
4. Serve with hot satay sauce and slices of cucumber and onions
5. Can also be served with noodles or rice
• Heating Instruction for Satay Sauce:
Empty satay sauce into pot and heat for 3 minutes at low heat
• If using Microwave:
Empty satay sauce into a microwave safe bowl and heat for 2 minutes

product.description:
Inspired by the authentic flavours from the street satays in Singapore, Golden Chef brings you the tantalising taste of our pork satay. Marinated to achieve charred perfection with its secret recipe, every bite is a memorable experience. Elevate your meals and gatherings with the juicy, BBQ-like goodness of this mouth-watering satay.

A great way to savour Singapore's heritage street food culture right at home, anytime.

metaData.Ingredients:
Ingredients For Pork Satay: Pork, Satay Marinade Sauce {Spice Paste (Turmeric, Shallot, Lemongrass, Ginger, Garlic, Galangal), Seasoning [Sugar, Charcoal Oil (Palm Olein, Synthetic Flavouring, Paprika Extract, Tocopherols (Antioxidant)), Cumin, Spices], Tartrazine (Colour)}, Vegetable Oil, Salt and Sodium Bicarbonate (Stabiliser).

Ingredients For Satay Sauce: Peanut, Water, Palm Oil [Palm Olein, Butylated Hydroxyanisole (Antioxidant), Polydimethylsiloxane (Anticaking Agent)], Sugar, Shallot, Onion, Turmeric, Chilli Paste [Chilli, Water, Salt, Benzoic Acid (Preservative)], Kecap Manis [Sugar, Water, Salt, Soy, Wheat, Sodium Benzoate (Preservative), Vegetable Stabiliser], Lemongrass, Garlic, Candlenut, Galangal, Spices and Chilli.

Manufactured In A Facility Which Also Processes Milk, Nuts, Gluten, Eggs, Peanuts, Soy, Fish and Crustacean.
metaData.Preparation:
Pan-Fry
1. Fully defrost the satay meat chunks before cooking
2. Lightly oil the pan and heat it over medium-low heat
3. Stir fry the defrosted meat chunks for 6 minutes or until fully cooked
Air-Fry or Oven-Grill
1. Fully defrost the satay meat chunks before cooking
2. Preheat the air fryer or oven to 200°C
3. Place the defrosted meat chunks in the air fryer or oven tray and cook for 10 minutes or until fully cooked

Heating Instructions for Satay Sauce:
1. Fully defrost the satay sauce
2. Empty defrosted satay sauce into a pot and heat it over low heat for 2 minutes or until it simmers
3. If using the microwave, empty defrosted satay sauce into a microwave-safe bowl and heat it for 45-60 seconds or until thoroughly heated

Serve cooked pork satay with satay sauce alongside with slices of cucumber and onions.
metaData.Storage Information:
• Keep frozen (-18°C & below).
• Once thawed, do not refreeze. Best consumed within 2 days from opening

product.name: Golden Chef Singapore Pork Satay - Unskewered

product.description:
• Tantalising Char-grilled Flavour
• Boldly Seasoned Tender Meat Cuts
• Includes Addictively Flavourful Chunky Peanut Sauce
• Proudly Made in Singapore

metaData.Ingredients:
Ingredients For Beef Satay: Beef, Sugar, Lemongrass, Turmeric, Kecap Manis [Sugar, Water, Salt, Soy, Wheat, Sodium Benzoate (Preservative), Vegetable Stabilizer], Shallot, Cumin, Charcoal Oil [Palm Olein, Synthetic Flavouring, Paprika Extract, Tocopherols (Antioxidant)], Palm Oil [Palm Olein, Butylated Hydroxyanisole (Antioxidant), Polydimethylsiloxane (Antifoaming Agent)], Ginger, Coriander, Baking Powder [Wheat Flour, Raising Agents (Sodium Bicarbonate, Disodium Diphosphate, Calcium Dihydrogen Phosphate)], Salt, Garlic, Galangal and Colours (Sunset Yellow Fcf, Tartrazine).

Ingredients For Satay Sauce: Peanut, Water, Palm Oil [Palm Olein, Butylated Hydroxyanisole (Antioxidant), Polydimethylsiloxane (Antifoaming Agent)], Sugar, Shallot, Onion, Turmeric, Chilli Paste [Chilli, Water, Salt, Benzoic Acid (Preservative)], Kecap Manis [Sugar, Water, Salt, Soy, Wheat, Sodium Benzoate (Preservative), Vegetable Stabilizer], Lemongrass, Garlic, Candlenut, Galangal, Spices and Chilli.
metaData.Key Information:
A first-of-its-kind collaboration with national monument and hawker icon Lau Pa Sat to bring authentic Satay Street flavours to Singaporean homes, Golden Chef offers tender meat cuts marinated with spices to achieve charred perfection.
Every bite is a delightful explosion of tantalising smoky aroma and juicy core, well-accompanied with the addictively flavourful chunky peanut sauce.
For families and individuals looking for nostalgic flavourful easy meals, Golden Chef satays provide a great way to savour the Lau Pa Sat Satay Street experience at home, anytime.
metaData.Preparation:
Pan-Fry
1. Fully defrost the satay meat chunks before cooking
2. Lightly oil the pan and heat it over medium-low heat
3. Stir fry the defrosted meat chunks for 6 minutes or until fully cooked

Air-Fry or Oven-Grill
1. Fully defrost the satay meat chunks before cooking
2. Preheat the air fryer or oven to 200°C
3. Place the defrosted meat chunks in the air fryer or oven tray and cook for 10 minutes or until fully cooked

Heating Instructions for Satay Sauce:
1. Fully defrost the satay sauce
2. Empty defrosted satay sauce into a pot and heat it over low heat for 2 minutes or until it simmers
3. If using the microwave, empty defrosted satay sauce into a microwave-safe bowl and heat it for 45-60 seconds or until thoroughly heated

Serve cooked chicken satay with satay sauce alongside with slices of cucumber and onions.
metaData.SAP Product Name: GOLDEN CHEF LAU PA SAT PAN FRY CHICKEN 300G
metaData.Storage Information:
Keep frozen (-18°C & below).
Once thawed, do not refreeze. Best consumed within 2 days from opening
